Steps.Message:
Sent by Will at 12:40 - I'm pleased to confirm that all power in the Lower Mardyke Avenue area should be back on. The issue was caused by an unexpected fire close to some of our equipment. Once it was deemed safe, our engineers cut away the damaged cabling which allowed them to replace some faulty network equipment and restore supplies.

We know that a power cut can be difficult, and I'm sorry for the length of the time you were without your electricity. Please rest assured that our staff will continue to work around the clock to keep the lights on.

If your power isn't on or there's anything you'd like to talk to us about, call us on 08003163105 which is free from landlines and mobiles.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
Thank you for your continued patience today.

We have head from our team on site who have advised that the emergency services have given us the all clear to proceed with our work. Our engineers will now expose the cabling leading to where the fire was and cut this away from the network, allowing them to restore power.

At this stage we're estimating to have the power back on between 12:30 and 13:30, although this time frame may change depending on how their work progresses. I will continue to keep you updated with any further news you may receive though. Sent at 12:07 by Will.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message: Sent by Will at 10:46 - We have spoken with our engineers and due to the damage caused by the fire, the emergency service has deemed it is still too unsafe for us to enter the site. Due to this we are currently unable to provide an accurate time frame for when the power will be back on. We're sorry for this, if you're worried about elderly or ill neighbours, we can offer help and support to our vulnerable customers during a power cut. Find out more here: https://UKPN.short.gy/PSR. You can also call us free on 105. We'll continue to update you as and when we receive any further information.

Sent by Will at 13:34 - We can now confirm that all power in the Lewes Road area should be back on. The issue was caused by an unexpected fault with some of our network equipment, which our engineers have now replaced.

We know that being without power can be especially difficult, so we're sorry for the outage. Please rest assured our staff will continue to work around the clock to keep the lights on.

If your power isn't on or there's anything you'd like to talk to us about, call us on 08003163105 which is free from landlines and mobiles.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
Sent by Will at 13:20 - Thanks for bearing with us today.

Our engineers are checking our substation equipment to ensure our network is healthy before they attempt to restore your power. They may also walk our cabling route throughout the area to check for any visible problems, check smaller electrical sites in the area, or apply specialist equipment which may restore power temporarily to help them with their testing.

We know that our customers may be worried about the food in the fridge and freezer, so we want to try and help with advice. Food should keep for up to 4 hours in the fridge, and between 24-48 hours in the freezer if you avoid opening them.

We'll keep you updated on our engineers progress.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message: Engineers have arrived on site to investigate what we believe to be an issue at the local substation in the Lewes Road area. Using an average timescale for this type of issue, we're looking to have you back on between 16:30 and 17:30 however we must stress that this time may change, depending on what our staff find. We know that a power cut can be very disruptive; we understand this can greatly impact your daily activities and we want to support you however we can. We'll update you with all of our progress, but for more information about this type of fault; here's a short YouTube video https://UKPN.short.gy/fuse. Sent by Will at 13:09.

We know some properties in the area may have faced other recent interruptions, and we're sorry for the extra disruption. Every outage is fully investigated by our engineers, though power may be lost several times before we can safely find and fix the issue. Our customer services team will keep as many customers updated as possible during these investigations.

To talk to us, or let us know that your power isn't restored, call us on 08003163105.

Hello, Kevin from the messaging team here. Thanks for your patience today. 

Our records show that some properties affected by today's power cut may have had other unexpected interruptions recently, and although we don't believe today's issue to be related, we are sorry for further disruption. Power cuts are fully investigated by our engineers, but can sometimes happen multiple times before we can safely find the issue and fix it.

You may not have had other power cuts recently, but if you have, our customer services team will try to update as many customers as possible about our investigations. 

Our staff are still working to get your power back on as quickly as they can, and we'll update you again soon.

Our engineers have arrived on site in the Romford RM3 8 area and are investigating the fault.

You may find your power is going on and off; that is normal for this type of issue and is due to our engineers working to divert power remotely. By doing this, we've managed to restore power to 1989 customers. There are now 455 properties still affected and using an average time frame we're estimating all power will be on between 18:00 and 19:00, but this may change later depending on what our staff find.

We'll keep sending SMS updates until everybody is restored. To stop receiving texts, just follow this link https://UKPN.short.gy/STOP to unsubscribe. Sent by Kevin at 16:55.

Steps.Message:
What will happen next?

This outage is affecting multiple areas, so locations we mention may be further away than you'd expect. We have already sent staff to the area, but at the same time we try to restore power remotely by redirecting the electricity around the faulty section. It's possible your power may go on and off as we try to restore as much power as we can.

The engineer will head to the local substation and after checking the network, will attempt to restore more power from there. If unsuccessful, they will locate and repair the issue, which will take longer to get the lights on.

Our staff work as quickly as they can, so you might not hear about everything we're doing, but we'll always try to give you the clearest information we have.

Logs.Comment:
Sent by Lisa at 00:03 - Thanks for bearing with us over today, I just wanted to give you the latest from our engineers.

Our staff have now confirmed that our substation equipment has operated due to an underground cable fault which we'll need to pinpoint, excavate, and repair.

We're sending specialist teams and equipment to site, and it's possible they will still be able to restore your power within the current time frame using other methods. However, once they've assessed further, we'll let you know if the time frame needs to change.

For more information about this type of fault; here's a short video https://UKPN.short.gy/UG - I'll keep you updated.

Sent by Lisa at 23:37 - Thanks for bearing with us today while our engineers work to restore the unplanned power cut affecting the Kings Meadow Road area. Our engineers have been able to divert electricity around the faulty part of the network, restoring power to some customers.

We know your power may now be back on, however; we'll keep sending text updates to everyone until all the power to your area is on. To stop receiving texts, just follow this link https://UKPN.short.gy/STOP to unsubscribe.
